title: cyrtomium macrophyllum
intro: Cyrtomium macrophyllum, also known as the netted polypody or maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to East Asia. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits associated with Cyrtomium macrophyllum include potential treatments for fever, sore throats, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a diuretic. In herbal preparations, the leaves and rhizomes of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its effects.
